color.description: Semantic Material Design 3 color roles and tonal surface tokens.

primary.summary: Primary accent role for key actions and highlighted states.

platform_notes.compose: Map to MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary and its matching on/container roles.
platform_notes.flutter: Map through ColorScheme primary and related container roles.
platform_notes.web: Expose as semantic CSS custom properties, not raw hex literals in components.

secondary.summary: Secondary accent role for lower-emphasis accent surfaces and supportive actions.

platform_notes.compose: Use when primary is already overloaded and the hierarchy needs another accent.
platform_notes.flutter: Map through ColorScheme secondary roles.
platform_notes.web: Use semantic aliases and preserve contrast with on-secondary.

tertiary.summary: Expressive accent role for complementary emphasis or highlight groupings.

platform_notes.compose: Use sparingly to preserve hierarchy.
platform_notes.flutter: Confirm component parity when applying tertiary accents.
platform_notes.web: Best used through explicit token aliases rather than direct component ownership.

surface-container-highest.summary: One of the stepped tonal surfaces used to separate elevated or grouped content without custom shades.

platform_notes.compose: Prefer stepped surfaces over manual cards-on-gray backgrounds.
platform_notes.flutter: Mirror stepped surfaces through tokenized theme colors.
platform_notes.web: Map to surface role variables to preserve tonal hierarchy.

outline.summary: Boundary and stroke role for medium-emphasis separators and outlines.

platform_notes.compose: Use for strokes when tonal separation alone is insufficient.
platform_notes.flutter: Keep outline usage tokenized and avoid arbitrary alpha borders.
platform_notes.web: Use for separators and field strokes with semantic aliases.

surface-tint.summary: Tint input used by MD3 elevation and tonal surface behavior.

platform_notes.compose: Use through system elevation behavior rather than manual tint stacking.
platform_notes.flutter: Keep tied to the theme model rather than local widget overrides.
platform_notes.web: Document as a system token even if runtime parity is partial.

typography.description: Material Design 3 semantic type scale roles.

headline-small.summary: High-emphasis screen or section heading role.

platform_notes.compose: Map to MaterialTheme.typography.headlineSmall.
platform_notes.flutter: Map to TextTheme.headlineSmall.
platform_notes.web: Expose through semantic text variables.

title-large.summary: Section and app bar title role.

platform_notes.compose: Prefer for app bars, cards, and grouped section headers.
platform_notes.flutter: Map to TextTheme.titleLarge.
platform_notes.web: Use for semantic heading treatment before custom sizes.

body-medium.summary: Default reading and supporting copy role.

platform_notes.compose: Default body role for dense content and supporting descriptions.
platform_notes.flutter: Map to TextTheme.bodyMedium.
platform_notes.web: Default prose token for content-heavy UI.

label-large.summary: Action and compact metadata label role.

platform_notes.compose: Use for button and tab labels.
platform_notes.flutter: Map to TextTheme.labelLarge.
platform_notes.web: Avoid replacing with arbitrary uppercase micro-type.

shape.description: System-level shape tokens for corners and rounded surfaces.

platform_notes.compose: Use through Shapes tokens, not per-widget literals.
platform_notes.flutter: Centralize in shape theme inputs.
platform_notes.web: Map to semantic radius variables.

corner-medium.summary: Default card and field rounding in many MD3 surfaces.

platform_notes.compose: Good baseline for cards and text fields.
platform_notes.flutter: Keep component families aligned to the same radius token.
platform_notes.web: Use as the default semantic container radius.

corner-large.summary: More expressive rounding for prominent surfaces.

platform_notes.compose: Use for larger sheets or prominent cards when justified.
platform_notes.flutter: Avoid mixing with unrelated radii in the same hierarchy.
platform_notes.web: Reserve for large surfaced containers.

corner-full.summary: Fully rounded pill shape for chips and related controls.

platform_notes.compose: Use for chip families and pill treatments.
platform_notes.flutter: Use only when the component family expects full rounding.
platform_notes.web: Avoid overusing outside explicit pill patterns.

elevation.description: Elevation levels coordinated with tonal surfaces.

level0.summary: Flat base surface with no separation.

platform_notes.compose: Prefer stepped surfaces before introducing shadow.
platform_notes.flutter: Zero elevation still requires correct surface roles.
platform_notes.web: Use surface tokens before shadow utilities.

level1.summary: Low emphasis separation for grouped surfaces.

platform_notes.compose: Use for subtle cards or raised controls.
platform_notes.flutter: Coordinate with ColorScheme surfaces.
platform_notes.web: Do not replace with arbitrary box-shadow stacks.

level3.summary: Pronounced separation for dialogs and prominent surfaces.

platform_notes.compose: Dialogs and sheets often live around this separation level.
platform_notes.flutter: Confirm component defaults before overriding.
platform_notes.web: Pair with tonal surfaces and shape tokens.

state-layers.description: State emphasis overlays and interaction feedback tokens.

platform_notes.compose: More relevant on large screens, ChromeOS, and desktop input modes.
platform_notes.flutter: Account for mouse and trackpad platforms.
platform_notes.web: Express with semantic state tokens, not one-off opacity values.

focus.summary: Focus emphasis state layer for keyboard or assistive navigation.

platform_notes.compose: Pair with focus semantics and visible outline where needed.
platform_notes.flutter: Ensure focus states remain discoverable in desktop/web builds.
platform_notes.web: Never remove visible focus without a tokenized replacement.

platform_notes.compose: Use through MD3 interaction tokens instead of local alpha hacks.
platform_notes.flutter: Preserve component feedback consistency across widgets.
platform_notes.web: Express through shared interaction variables.

dragged.summary: Dragged state emphasis for movable surfaces.

platform_notes.compose: Relevant when drag-and-drop or reordering is present.
platform_notes.flutter: Use only when the interaction exists.
platform_notes.web: Avoid inventing it for non-draggable controls.
